hero.subtitle: Expert Consulting in High-Voltage Cable Systems & Offshore Wind Energy
hero.tagline: 50+ years of pioneering research. 110+ projects delivered. IEEE Prize Paper Award 2019.

about.heading: A Lifetime of Innovation in Cable Engineering
about.lead: Prof. Dr.-Ing. habil. Heinrich Brakelmann is one of the world's foremost authorities on high-voltage power cable systems — combining five decades of academic research with hands-on delivery of more than 110 major energy infrastructure projects.
about.bio1: He studied electrical engineering at RWTH Aachen (Diploma 1971) and completed his doctoral thesis on circuit breakers in 1973. He then joined Felten & Guilleaume Energietechnik AG in Cologne, leading an R&D unit focused on high-power cable technology — a role that grounded his theoretical expertise in large-scale industrial practice.
about.bio2: From the 1980s onward, Brakelmann held the Chair for Power Cable Technology at the University of Duisburg-Essen, where he served as Professor at the Institute for Energy Transport and Storage (IW/ETS) for over two decades. Now Professor emeritus, he continues to publish and consult at the frontier of the field.
about.bio3: Through BCC Cable Consulting, he brings this unmatched combination of academic depth and project experience to clients worldwide — from offshore wind farm developers to national grid operators.
about.award1Desc: For "Rating of Underground Power Cables with Boundary Temperature Restrictions" — IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
about.award2Desc: VDE/ETG Award for outstanding contributions to power cable technology
about.award3Desc: Long-standing contributions to CIGRE Working Groups B1 on cable systems
about.affiliation: Professor emeritus, Institute for Energy Transport and Storage (IW/ETS), Faculty of Engineering Sciences, University of Duisburg-Essen

projects.heading: 110+ Projects Delivered
projects.subheading: A selection of representative engagements across offshore and onshore cable systems.
projects.confidentialityNote: Project details available upon request — client confidentiality applies.

northSeaOffshore.title: North Sea Offshore Wind Farm, 2019
northSeaOffshore.description: End-to-end inpark grid layout for a 400 MW offshore wind farm in the North Sea. Included cable routing, cross-section selection, and connection to the offshore substation.

onshoreHV.title: Inland HV Transmission Corridor, 2021
onshoreHV.description: Integration of a 220 kV underground cable system into an existing overhead transmission corridor. Detailed thermal analysis and system compatibility study.

submarineThermal.title: Deepwater Export Cable Study, 2018
submarineThermal.description: Thermal rating and transient load analysis for a 100 km submarine export cable connecting an offshore wind farm to the mainland grid.

magneticShielding.title: Urban Underground Cable Route, 2022
magneticShielding.description: EMF impact assessment and magnetic shielding design for a high-current cable route passing beneath a densely populated urban area.

hvCables.title: High-Voltage Cable Systems
hvCables.description: Construction, optimization, and system integration of high-voltage cable systems. From design through commissioning, we deliver reliable solutions for onshore and submarine power transmission.

offshore.title: Offshore Wind Park Cabling
offshore.description: Inpark grid layout and connection to the transmission grid for offshore wind farms. Comprehensive cabling concepts that optimize cost, reliability, and power delivery.

thermal.title: Thermal Analysis & Cable Rating
thermal.description: Transient and stationary current ratings, heating behavior of submarine and underground cables. Precise thermal modeling ensures safe operation at maximum capacity.

emf.title: EMF & Magnetic Shielding
emf.description: Analysis of electric, magnetic, and thermal fields around cable systems. Expert assessment of EMF exposure and design of effective shielding measures.

transmission.title: Transmission Behavior Analysis
transmission.description: Transient and stationary behavior analysis of cables and overhead lines. Detailed modeling of switching surges, lightning impacts, and steady-state performance.

layout.title: Wind Park Cable Layout Optimization
layout.description: Optimized cabling layout with respect to technology, economics, and reliability. Minimizing cable lengths and losses while maximizing energy yield.

monitoring.title: Monitoring & Forecasting Systems
monitoring.description: Intelligent monitoring with predictive forecasting for cable infrastructure. Real-time condition assessment and proactive maintenance planning for critical assets.
